name: ProcessWire CMS REST API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP CMS with REST API — enabling developers to build highly customized content management systems with a flexible field and template model, headless delivery via ProcessWire's built-in selectors API and optional REST API modules, with MySQL/MariaDB storage and a reputation for exceptional flexibility and developer control.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via ProcessWire REST API module for decoupled frontend applications
Managing ProcessWire pages and fields via API for content migration and automation
Querying ProcessWire content via selector language API for complex content filtering
Building custom ProcessWire API endpoints via PHP for specialized content delivery
Managing ProcessWire users and permissions via API for CMS user provisioning
not_for: Teams without PHP expertise (ProcessWire is a PHP CMF — requires developer)
Teams needing managed SaaS CMS without server administration
Non-technical content editors without developer setup (requires PHP hosting)
best_when: A PHP developer or agency needs maximum flexibility to build a completely custom CMS — where every field, template, and relationship is developer-defined, and ProcessWire's selector syntax provides powerful querying without SQL.
avoid_when: You don't have PHP expertise, need managed SaaS, want out-of-box headless API without PHP modules, or need a large ecosystem.

auth.notes: REST API via optional ProcessWire REST API module (third-party). API key header auth via the REST module. Native ProcessWire session auth (cookie-based) for built-in access. No OAuth2. No built-in webhooks — use custom PHP hooks or module. ProcessWire's Selectors API is the primary programmatic interface (PHP, not HTTP). HTTP REST via module requires installation. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (MPL 2.0 license)
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers — community donation supported
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Ryan Cramer in 2010. MPL 2.0 license. GitHub: processwire/processwire (4,700+ stars). PHP 7.4+ + MySQL/MariaDB. Known as the 'developer's CMS' for flexibility. No opinionated content model — everything is custom fields and templates. Selectors syntax for powerful content querying. Module ecosystem (400+ modules) for features. Used by agencies building custom client CMS solutions. No REST API out-of-box — requires module installation. ProcessWire Rocks community. Ryan Cramer actively maintains.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API requires module installation. Primary API is PHP-level Selectors. No webhooks out-of-box. Open source. PHP expertise required. Self-hosted — no rate limits. No OAuth2. No managed SaaS option. Smaller community than WordPress/Drupal. Module quality varies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page reads idempotent by ID or path. Field reads idempotent. Write operations idempotent by page ID. Selector queries idempotent for same parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: No built-in REST API — must install ProcessWire REST API module (third-party)
No built-in HTTP webhooks — requires custom PHP hooks or modules
PHP expertise required — Selectors API is PHP-only by default
Module quality varies — evaluate REST modules before adopting
Self-hosted only — no managed cloud option

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. API key auth via REST module. PHP session auth for native access. No OAuth2. Module-based security — evaluate REST module security. Open source — auditable PHP code. Ryan Cramer active maintenance. User responsible for server hardening. No compliance certifications.
